entry.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
entry.content:
Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Zoom CISO: AI as a Security Enabler, Not Role-Replacer
entry.content:
Zoom CISO Sandra McLeod discusses the challenges of securing a global communication platform, the promise of AI-driven security workflows, and her advice for aspiring cybersecurity leaders.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
entry.content:
Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
entry.content:
China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
entry.content:
Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
entry.content:
Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
entry.content:
Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
entry.content:
One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
entry.content:
AI worms, or "viruses with wings and brains," adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
entry.content:
Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million
entry.content:
The ShinyHunters extortion group leaked roughly 234 GB of data allegedly stolen from the dental benefits administrator. The post Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: In Other News: Anthropic Maps AI Threats, Unpatched Comodo Flaw, Palantir Chief Eyed for CISA
entry.content:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Ultrahuman data leak, The Gentlemen ransomware analysis, Hola Browser bundles miner. The post In Other News: Anthropic Maps AI Threats, Unpatched Comodo Flaw, Palantir Chief Eyed for CISA appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Opal Security Raises $23 Million for AI-Native Identity Governance
entry.content:
Raising $59 million to date, Opal also announced five senior leadership appointments. The post Opal Security Raises $23 Million for AI-Native Identity Governance appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Emphere Raises $2.1 Million for AI-Powered Vulnerability Remediation
entry.content:
Emphere’s solution delivers AI-driven remediation to software companies to speed up releases. The post Emphere Raises $2.1 Million for AI-Powered Vulnerability Remediation appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Meta Says 20,000 Instagram Accounts Hacked via AI Tool Abuse
entry.content:
The social media giant has informed authorities about the impact of the recent attack involving an account recovery support tool. The post Meta Says 20,000 Instagram Accounts Hacked via AI Tool Abuse appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: SolarWinds Serv-U Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
entry.content:
Unauthenticated attackers can exploit the flaw via specially crafted POST requests that crash the Serv-U service. The post SolarWinds Serv-U Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls
entry.content:
The Active Sessions and Lockdown Mode features are being made more broadly available by the AI giant. The post OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks
entry.content:
Focusing on hacking law firms in the US, the ransomware group relies on fast flux to hide its C&C infrastructure. The post Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russia seeks to label two anti-Kremlin hacker groups as ‘extremist’
entry.content:
The groups have previously claimed responsibility for cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure and government institutions in Russia and Belarus.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
entry.content:
A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
entry.content:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
entry.content:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
entry.content:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
entry.content:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
entry.content:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Patient Data Exposed in Cyberattacks on Dental Practices
entry.content:
Data breaches have been announced by Bridle Trails Family Dentistry, Verber Dental Group, and Bronsky Orthodontics. Across the three incidents, […] The post Patient Data Exposed in Cyberattacks on Dental Practices appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Family Medicine Centers Pay $2.15M to Resolve Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
FMC Services, LLC, which does business as Family Medicine Centers in Texas, has agreed to a $2,150,000 settlement to resolve […] The post Family Medicine Centers Pay $2.15M to Resolve Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Parents Sue Minnesota Hospital to Enforce HIPAA Right of Access for Minor Child’s Medical Records
entry.content:
The parents of a 15-year-old child have filed a lawsuit against a Minnesota hospital for failing to provide them with […] The post Parents Sue Minnesota Hospital to Enforce HIPAA Right of Access for Minor Child’s Medical Records appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Singing River Health System: 54K Individuals Affected by December Cyberattack
entry.content:
Singing River Health System in Mississippi has issued an update on a cybersecurity incident that was first announced in December […] The post Singing River Health System: 54K Individuals Affected by December Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: $3.3M Settlement Resolves Data Breach Lawsuit Against Mt. Baker Imaging & Northwest Radiologists
entry.content:
Mt. Baker Imaging and Northwest Radiologists have agreed to pay $3,300,000 to settle a consolidated class action lawsuit stemming from […] The post $3.3M Settlement Resolves Data Breach Lawsuit Against Mt. Baker Imaging & Northwest Radiologists appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026
entry.content:
The HIPAA Journal has compiled healthcare data breach statistics from October 2009, when the Department of Health and Human Services […] The post Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Clarinda Regional Health Center Reports Data Breach Affecting 24K Patients
entry.content:
Data breaches have been announced by Clarinda Regional Health Center in Iowa, Community Connections in DC, Waveny Lifecare Network in […] The post Clarinda Regional Health Center Reports Data Breach Affecting 24K Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Conduent Business Services Data Breach Affected More Than 62.2 Million Individuals
entry.content:
In January 2025, news first surfaced about a massive data breach at Conduent Business Services, a vendor that provides printing, […] The post Conduent Business Services Data Breach Affected More Than 62.2 Million Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Onsite Women’s Health $2.5M Data Breach Settlement
entry.content:
A breach of the email account of an employee of Onsite Women’s Health that exposed the protected health information of […] The post Onsite Women’s Health $2.5M Data Breach Settlement appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Hacking Group Claims Responsibility for Multi-Million-Record DentaQuest Data Breach
entry.content:
Wellesley, MA-based DentaQuest, a dental benefits administrator that manages the benefits for 32 million Americans, has announced it is actively […] The post Hacking Group Claims Responsibility for Multi-Million-Record DentaQuest Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Microsoft’s incident response is getting a failing grade from researchers (1)
entry.content:
Microsoft is ticking off a lot of researchers this week by claiming that those who dump proof-of-concept exploits for vulnerabilities they have not responsibly disclosed are enabling criminal activity, and that Microsoft will track them and bring cases against them. Whoever advised them to issue that statement may want to walk it back. (See update... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Bombay High Court Issues Injunction Prohibiting Hackers From Publishing Allegedly Hacked HDFC Investor Data (1)
entry.content:
The Bombay High Court granted interim relief to HDFC AMC after a ransomware group called “Morpheus” allegedly stole over 680 GB of sensitive company and investor data. The court barred unidentified hackers from publishing or sharing the information, warning that any leak could lead to identity theft, financial fraud and irreparable harm. The case will... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Alberto Daniel Hill’s Cybermidnight Coverage of the Latin American Digital Sovereignty Crisis (March–June 2026)
entry.content:
Alberto Daniel Hill’s report is a must-read for anyone who wants to begin to understand what is going on in Argentina, Uruguay, and Mexico with respect to digital security. One of the many limitations of being a solo blogger is that there are entire areas of the world or sectors I basically know nothing about... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked
entry.content:
Jason Koebler reports: Hackers say that they used Meta’s AI support chatbot to break into a host of high-profile Instagram profiles by asking the support bot to change the email address associated with the target account. The claims coincide with a series of high-profile Instagram account takeovers, including the Barack Obama White House account, the Chief Master... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Data of 600,000 Gaza households exposed in World Food Programme cyberattack
entry.content:
Jacob Goldberg and Irwin Loy report: A cyber-attack targeting the World Food Programme has exposed sensitive personal information belonging to some 600,000 households in Gaza, the UN’s food agency has confirmed, in what may be the largest-known breach of humanitarian beneficiary data to date. WFP is investigating a “security-related incident” in which “unauthorised actors” accessed... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Most organizations that miss 24-hour patch window report breaches
entry.content:
Steve Zurier reports: The Cloud Security Alliance (CSA) found that 80% of organizations that miss the 24-hour patch window report security incidents involving known vulnerabilities. CSA’s study, released June 2, also found that even pre-production controls are not stopping known flaws in the AI age as 82% of organizations lack real-time visibility into AI runtime behavior.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: KR: Tving CEO Apologizes for Unprecedented Data Leak
entry.content:
This is what incident response and accountability should look like in the U.S., too, but almost never does. The Chosun Daily reports: OTT platform Tving, TVING, has faced controversy over leaking members’ personal information, with its representative director personally apologizing. On the afternoon of the 3rd, Tving’s CEO Choi Joo-hee stated, “We sincerely apologize for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Was “ExPresidents” a real hacker or a fabricated account?
entry.content:
DataBreaches recently recommended an article by Alberto Daniel Hill about digital security in Argentina, Uruguay, and Mexico. In describing his article, DataBreaches reported: In one section of his report, Hill calls out a company for allegedly manufacturing cyber threats, which he claims they then use to create public panic through media amplification. With the public... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Ex-Threat Intel Exec Accuses IBM and AT&T of Hiding Hacks
entry.content:
Tiffany Wang reports: IBM and AT&T lacked basic security controls and hid nation-state hacking breaches from the government, a former IBM threat intelligence official alleged in a newly unsealed lawsuit. Former IBM Vice President of Threat Intelligence William Barlow claimed the companies did not keep logs for AT&T-managed VPN connections into IBM cloud services and... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Silent Ransom Group (SRG): Uncovering DNS Fast Flux Infrastructure
entry.content:
In 2022, DataBreaches wondered whether a group with no name might be the most successful group we had never heard about. Our impression that the group was unique was somewhat confirmed in 2024, when it walked away from a ransom offer of $1.8 million. More recently, the group, now commonly referred to as the “Silent... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hola Browser for Windows compromised to deliver cryptominer
entry.content:
The Windows version of the Hola Browser has been compromised in a supply chain attack that delivered an undeclared executable identified by researchers as a cryptocurrency miner. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Cisco warns of unpatched SD-WAN zero-day exploited in attacks
entry.content:
On Thursday, Cisco warned of a high-severity, unpatched zero-day in the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (tracked as CVE-2026-20245) actively exploited in attacks enabling root privilege escalation. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: What 2026 DBIR Confirms: Attacks Are Living in the Browser
entry.content:
Phishing, shadow AI, malicious extensions, and credential theft increasingly happen inside the browser. Keep Aware explains what the 2026 Verizon DBIR reveals about browser-layer security gaps and modern attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Over 900 US gas station tank gauge systems exposed to attacks
entry.content:
Over 900 automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems across the United States, used to monitor fuel and chemical storage tanks across various critical infrastructure sectors, have been found exposed online and are vulnerable to ongoing attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Chinese APT deploys new malware to keep access to hacked networks
entry.content:
A Chinese espionage group tracked as UNC5221 has been accessing Microsoft 365 environments using the Brickstorm backdoor and previously undocumented malware named Plenet and AgentPSD. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CISA: Hackers now exploit SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers
entry.content:
CISA warned today that hackers are now actively exploiting a recently patched high-severity SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Over 20,000 Instagram accounts stolen in Meta AI support hack
entry.content:
Meta has revealed that 20,225 Instagram users had their accounts hijacked in a recent incident where attackers used Meta's AI-powered support system to reset passwords. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: ERMI LLC
entry.content:
ERMI LLC reported a data breach in California.
📍 Location: California | 📰 Source: California AG | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Interstate Management Company, LLC
entry.content:
Interstate Management Company, LLC reported a data breach in California.
📍 Location: California | 📰 Source: California AG | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Nickey Kehoe
entry.content:
Nickey Kehoe reported a data breach in California.
